CREATE TABLE test_table (id UInt32, value String);
SELECT id, toDecimal128OrNull(value, 2) AS decimal_value FROM test_table WHERE id <= 3;
SELECT id, toDecimal128OrNull(value, 10) AS decimal_value FROM test_table WHERE value LIKE '-%';
SELECT DISTINCT toDecimal128OrNull(value, 5) AS decimal_value FROM test_table WHERE value NOT LIKE 'Inf' AND value NOT LIKE 'NaN';
WITH (SELECT AVG(toDecimal128OrNull(value, 2)) FROM test_table WHERE id < 4) AS avg_value SELECT toDecimal128OrNull(toString(avg_value), 2) AS rounded_avg;
SELECT id, toDecimal128OrNull(CAST(value AS String), 38) AS decimal_value FROM test_table WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE value LIKE '%0.001%');
CREATE TABLE second_test_table (num UInt32, desc String);
SELECT a.id, a.value, b.desc, toDecimal128OrNull(a.value, 3) AS decimal_value FROM test_table AS a JOIN second_test_table AS b ON a.id = b.num;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT toDecimal128OrNull(value, 1) AS decimal_value FROM test_table GROUP BY id HAVING decimal_value IS NOT NULL);
